vulnerability.reference: Users uncritically accept biased classifications, especially when decisions affect unfamiliar or minority groups.
vulnerability.generated: End-users depend on an adaptive classifier whose human-inspired memory biases privilege recent classes, leaving earlier ones susceptible to systematic neglect.

impact.reference: Disproportionate errors for less frequent classes harm minority users through unfair decisions.
impact.generated: Real-time decisions based on misclassified earlier classes cause operational errors and erode trust in the adaptive system.

dose_response.reference: Skewed retention from recency biases causes systematic prediction errors, which when end-users rely on real-time adaptive classification lead to uncritical acceptance of biased outputs, disproportionately harming minority users.
dose_response.generated: Recency and primacy biases in continual learning architectures, through end-users relying on adaptive real-time classification, trigger systematic neglect of earlier classes, leading to misclassification-induced operational errors and eroded trust.
